caption:
‼️and has one of the strangest buildings in Europe 🤯

🤍 Follow for more unique places around the world @tripuntraveled

This place is known as the birthplace of Salvador Dalí, but the town itself feels like one of his creations.

The Dalí Theatre Museum is one of the strangest places in Europe. Giant eggs on the roof, golden sculptures on the walls, hidden illusions around every corner, and Dalí himself is buried beneath.

Even if you’re not into art, this place makes you question what’s real and what’s part of the performance.

This is…

📍 Figueres, Catalonia, Spain

🚗 How to get here
Figueres is about 1 hour from Barcelona by high speed train or 90 minutes by car. It’s an easy day trip or stop on a Costa Brava road trip.

What to eat
Try suquet de peix (Catalan fish stew), local cured meats, and crema catalana for dessert.

📌 Save this for your next Spain trip

caption:
I never grew up thinking, “One day, I’ll be a content creator.” 

👩‍💻I was supposed to follow the safe path.
I went to school, got my degree, landed my 9-5 job in the advanced air mobility industry and I’m VERY proud of that. I get to be part of very few people shaping the future of air mobility. 

But deep down… I always felt this quiet pull towards something more. I always find myself chasing little pockets of the world that most people skip or haven’t even heard of! The places not trending on Instagram and tiny villages where you get lost on purpose.

So I picked up my camera 📸 and started sharing stories through not just places, but emotions. Slowly, my small corner of Instagram grew into a beautiful community of people who are always looking for something unique or different.

✅ I want to be a content creator to inspire 
people who feel stuck, 
people who think travel isn’t for them,
people who think they need to quit their jobs to see the world. 
You don’t. I didn’t.
✅ You can chase your dreams without sacrificing stability.
✅ You can start before you’re “ready.”
‼️ You can live fully even if the world tells you to choose one path.

It’s possible to build a life where one day, I can turn this into something even bigger ✈️ my own business, my own brand, my own freedom. Remember, “you don’t need to be great to start but you NEED TO start in order to be great”. 

and if one person watches my content and thinks “Wait… maybe I can do this too”..that’s the real win

The Decameron by Giovanni Boccaccio was written right here in this tiny Tuscan hilltop town during the Black Death in 1353. 

This earthy realism book is a collection of 100 scandalous stories and tales that were so outrageous it got banned across countries.

Boccaccio later inspired Shakespeare, Chaucer, and now Netflix.

This is…

📍 Certaldo Alto, Tuscany, Italy

🚗 How to get here
It is easily accessible by car or you can take a train to Certaldo. Certaldo Alto sits on top of Certaldo Basso so it’s a steep walk uphill or you can take a funicular.

🧁 What to eat
Local Certaldo red onion dishes are a must try here. They even have ice cream in that flavor

📌 Save this for your Tuscany trip because this one is worth it.

Did you know The Decameron was written here?

This city was once one of the richest places on Earth and thrived as one of Europe’s most important trading hubs. 

Ships from across the continent sailed into its canals, bringing wealth that transformed it into a medieval powerhouse.

Then the waterways slowly silted up, trade moved elsewhere, money disappeared, and then centuries of decline while the rest of Europe modernized around it.

Now there are medieval towers, cobblestone streets, hidden canals, horse drawn carriages, and some of the most beautiful architecture in Europe almost exactly as it looked hundreds of years ago.

This is…

📍 Bruges, Belgium

🚗 How to get here
Bruges is about 1 hour by train from Brussels and an easy day trip, though I’d recommend staying overnight to experience the city after the crowds leave.

🍫 What to eat
Belgian waffles, fresh fries, local chocolates, and a warm bowl of Flemish beef stew.

caption:
‼️if you are visiting Venice this year👇🏻 

🤍 Follow for more unique places and travel guides around the world

Getting there

Fly into Venice Marco Polo Airport. From there, you can take a water taxi (expensive but iconic) or the Alilaguna water bus (budget-friendly). 

If you’re coming by train, Venezia Santa Lucia drops you straight into the heart of the city.

Where to stay

If it’s your first time, stay near San Marco or along the Grand Canal so you can walk everywhere early in the morning before crowds take over.

🤍 I stayed at the @stregisvenice and it was an epic experience. One of my favorite moments of staying here was that every evening they carry on a ritual called champagne sabering - where a bottle is opened with a sword (yes, an actual sword) and I actually got to do it myself and then try it 🤍

Things to do

Get lost. Not even kidding! Venice is best experienced with no plan
Wake up early and walk through Piazza San Marco before 8 AM
Take a vaporetto or gondola ride down the Grand Canal
Visit Burano for colorful houses or Murano for glassmaking
Climb the Campanile for one of the best views in the city

What to eat

Skip the tourist places right by the main squares.
Walk a few streets away and look for smaller, local spots.
Try cicchetti (Venetian tapas), fresh pasta, and tiramisu (this is where it was born).
Also, don’t leave without having a spritz by the water.

Things no one tells you ⚠️

Venice is beautiful, but it’s also crowded. Early mornings and late evenings are your best friend
Restaurants near major attractions are often overpriced and not worth it
Pack light! There are a LOT of bridges and no Ubers here
Acqua alta (flooding) can happen, especially in certain months, so check ahead

This fairytale town has heart shaped cutouts on the windows…and centuries ago, they weren’t just for decoration.

In medieval times, a heart carved into the wooden shutters meant a girl of marriageable age lived in that house.

No heart usually meant the “listing” was taken or the family wasn’t open to offers 🤫

This is…

📍 Colmar, France

caption:
In the 1800s, 1 in every 3 clocks in the entire world came from this tiny German town.

It started with poor farmers who couldn’t inherit land and had nothing to do during the long snowed in winters. So they started carving clocks by hand just to survive.

By 1808, there were already hundreds of clockmakers in just this one small region alone and their clocks were being sold across Europe, America and Russia.

This is…

📍 Triberg, Black Forest, Germany

The best way to explore Triberg and the surrounding Black Forest is by car. Roads are scenic and well connected. From Freiburg (yes the canal marriage place 😂) it’s about an hour, from Stuttgart around 2 hrs.

Save this if Germany is on your list

caption:
‼️ all details👇🏻 

This hilltop town in Europe is older than Rome…and parts of it are literally collapsing into the earth.

Over 2,500 years ago, the Etruscans built this city on soft clay cliffs in Tuscany.

Today, those cliffs are slowly eroding, and entire sections of ancient walls have already fallen into the valley below.

And yet…it’s one of the most peaceful and beautiful towns in Tuscany.

This is…

📍 Volterra, Tuscany, Italy

Here’s how to spend 1 perfect day here

9 AM - Enter through Porta all’Arco and walk the ancient walls.

10 AM - Explore Piazza dei Priori which is one of the oldest town halls in Tuscany. Browse the alabaster shops, and just explore the old town.

12 PM - Lunch overlooking the valley.

1:30 PM - Visit the Roman Theatre ruins just outside the walls which is incredibly well preserved and rarely crowded.

3:00 PM - Check out the balze cliffs if you have time

Sunset
Go to viewpoint along the walls.

caption:
This Italian village has colorful pastel houses so the sailors wouldn’t go to the wrong home and wrong wife.

Back in the days, the fishermen would spend months at sea and when they returned, the coastline looked almost identical.

So families painted their homes in bold, distinct colors with unique window details so they could recognize them from the water.

This is…

📍 Camogli, Liguria, Italy

🤍 Follow for unique places, road trips, and places most people don’t know about  @tripuntraveled 

Some even believe the name Camogli comes from Casa delle Mogli which means houses of the wives.

🚗 How to get here
Train from Genoa or La Spezia. The station drops you right above the harbor. If you are driving, there is quite some parking available outside the village.

Would you trust a man who needs color coding to find their home? 😂😭

caption:
‼️and it’s a wild story👇🏻

🤍 Follow for more unique places around the world @tripuntraveled

this looks like one of the most magical towns in Italy but the famous cone-shaped houses here weren’t originally built to impress

they were constructed using a technique that allowed them to be dismantled quickly whenever tax inspectors came through the region

Centuries later, what started as a clever workaround became one of the most unique towns in Europe and a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

If you’re road-tripping through Puglia, this place is absolutely worth adding to your itinerary.

This is…

📍 Alberobello, Puglia, Italy

🚗 How to get here

Alberobello is easiest to visit by car as part of a Puglia road trip.

From Bari, it’s about a 1-hour drive. From Polignano a Mare, Monopoli, or Ostuni, it’s around 30–45 minutes.

If you don’t have a car, take a train from Bari to Alberobello with a transfer, or use a direct bus from Bari.

🅿️ There are several paid parking lots just outside the historic center, then it’s a short walk into town.

caption:
Utah really is one of the most stunning places in the entire country!! 😍

If you are planning a trip to this amazing part of the USA, make sure to follow @peekinourjournal and see more bucketlist spots and destinations to have the best time out here ❤️

↩️ Arches National Park- One of the most famous national parks in the country. It boasts amazing natural arches, including Delicate Arch which is Utah’s License Plate Symbol
♦️ Canyonlands National Park- Spectacular park with amazing views! Catching the sunrise at Mesa Arch when it glows red, has to be on your list
🐎 Dead Horse Point State Park- One of the best sunrise spots in Moab, this state park deserves so much more love! The entry fee is $20 and we highly recommend visiting this place on your trip to Moab
🛻 Marlboro Point- This hidden gem is only accessible via a 4x4 vehicle and offers jaw dropping views across deep, rugged canyons
🏜️ Goblin Valley State Park- Red rocks and rugged hoodoos dominate the landscape of this beautiful park. You can purchase a drone permit at the entrance station 
👽 Bentonite Hills- Located near Mars Research station, this otherworldly spot will surely make you feel like you transported to another planet
🏰 Capitol Reef National Park- The least visited National Park in Utah, this destination is a hidden gem.There are stunning views everywhere and park offers some amazing hikes and even world class rappelling!
🪨 Factory Butte- This beautiful monolith overlooks Hanksville and can be seen from the highway. We suggest off roading and getting closer to it as the landscape is just surreal 
🌒 Moon Overlook- Otherworldly landscape which looks so much like the surface of the Moon. Best accessed via a 4x4, this spot is located close to Hanksville

🧊 Glacier Lagoon- Lagoon full of icebergs where you can kayak
💦 Svartifoss- Gorgeous waterfall with Basalt columns 
🌳 Fjaðrárgljúfur Canyon- Stunning canyon with grey rocks and lush scenery
🌳 Mulagljufur Canyon- Green and lush, this canyon has great scenery and waterfalls
⚫️ Vestrahorn- Black sand beach with picture perfect mountains
💦 Hengifoss- Gorgeous waterfall with colorful rocks 
🛘 Studlagil- Canyon made of basalt columns
♨️ Myvatn- Town located near an accessible geo thermal area
🐳 Husavik- Small town famous for world class whale watching

caption:
If you are planning to visit Utah and are impacted by the new National Park entry rules, these must visit spots outside the National Parks are the perfect way to experience Utah 🫶😍

✨Hi! We’re Palak and Vaibhav, a couple based in eastern USA who loves to travel and enjoy the beautiful outdoors. Follow along our journey if you like to hike, go on roadtrips and travel the world with your partner ♥️

🪨 Factory Butte- This otherworldly monolith is 6,300 ft or 1,920 m high and you can drive very close to it with a bit of off roading
🚘 Potash Road- Stunning drive going through a beautiful red rock canyon with massive red rock wall on one side and the Colorado River on the other
🌈 Bentonite Hills- A stunning display of colors makes this spot really special. It is nearby NASA’s Mars Desert Research Station in Hanksville
🌘 Moon Overlook- Also called Moonscape Overlook or Skyline Rim Overlook, this spot will really make you think you’re on the moon
🛻 Marlboro Point- This amazing hidden gem is accessible via a 4x4 if you want to make it all the way to the point. You can opt to park around 0.4 mile away if you are more comfortable with that
🏜️ Goblin Valley State Park- Dense hoodoos define the surreal landscape of this beautiful park. A $20 fee is required for this state park
🗼 Long Dong Silver- A strikingly beautiful Spire in Hanksville, Utah. Requires ~1 mile hike to reach this spot
🐎 Dead Horse Point State Park- One of the best sunrise we have ever seen was inside this park. The warm light of the sunrise illuminates the red rocks with a striking red glow. We highly recommend a visit here
